title: [InstCombine] Drop nsw in negation of select

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary specifically mentions nsw flag handling, but nuw (no unsigned wrap) is a different poison-generating flag not explicitly covered in the documentation

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i32 @negate_select_nuw_flag(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %c) {
  %t1 = select i1 %c, i32 0, i32 %x
  %t2 = sub i32 %y, %t1
  ret i32 %t2
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @negate_select_nuw_flag(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %c) {
#0:
  %t0 = sub nuw i32 0, %x
  %t1 = select i1 %c, i32 %t0, i32 %x
  %t2 = sub i32 %y, %t1
  ret i32 %t2
}
=>
define i32 @negate_select_nuw_flag(i32 %x, i32 %y, i1 %c) {
#0:
  %t1 = select i1 %c, i32 0, i32 %x
  %t2 = sub i32 %y, %t1
  ret i32 %t2
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions dropping nsw flags, but doesn't document the case where multiple poison-generating flags (nsw + nuw combined) are present on the negation instruction

source_filename = "<stdin>"

  %t2 = sub i16 %y, %t1
  ret i16 %t2
}
